title: transport.rs: parse_incoming defined after #[cfg(test)] block
description: In core/src/transport.rs, the function parse_incoming (line 606) is defined after the #[cfg(test)] mod tests block (lines 502-604). While this compiles correctly (parse_incoming is in the top-level module, not inside the test module), the placement is confusing: a reader scanning the file sees the test module appear to be the last thing, then finds a non-test function after it. Standard Rust convention is to place all non-test code before the test module. Low-risk cosmetic issue but could cause a reviewer to think parse_incoming is test-only.
